mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in the federal funds rate raise risk-free discount rates and corporate borrowing costs, mechanically reducing the present value of future equity cash flows and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS 3-month change exhibits a positive Rank IC with 20-day SPX returns over any 3-year rolling window, or mean forward return during tightening periods is non-negative.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating aggregate demand and forthcoming earnings downgrades, prompting risk-off repositioning that generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE has risen more than 0.3 percentage points over the preceding 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent contraction in retail sales and industrial production indicates a broad slump spanning final demand and production, leading investors to mark down earnings estimates and triggering negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint z-score combination of RSAFS and INDPRO fails to predict negative 60-day SPX returns with Rank IC below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production momentum during early-cycle recoveries reflects inventory restocking and rising capacity utilization that lifts cyclical earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO momentum generates positive 5-day SPX returns in fewer than 52 percent of post-recession recovery months over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in real retail sales demonstrates robust consumer spending that directly translates into higher corporate revenues and supports positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: RSAFS 3-month trend exhibits negative Rank IC with 20-day SPX returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth coinciding with a low unemployment rate signals an overheating labor market that accelerates wage costs and corporate margin compression, producing negative quarterly S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change exceeds 400k and UNRATE is below 5.5%
failure_condition: Joint signal of strong PAYEMS and low UNRATE is followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 50 percent of instances during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A negative month-over-month change in industrial production triggers an immediate downward revision to near-term earnings expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO prints coincide with positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55 percent of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal strengthening residential investment and construction activity that boosts demand for cyclical inputs and labor, lifting broad equity prices and generating positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: HOUST 3-month momentum fails to achieve positive Rank IC with 20-day SPX returns over any 3-year rolling window.
